user_query:
I’m planning a small social campaign to celebrate my friend’s art (we’ve been friends since middle school), and I want to make sure the posts are on-message and accessible. Please use the inputs under input/ to create platform-specific posts and a concise campaign summary, then add and run a simple validator so I can verify everything locally.

What I need:
1) Read these files:
   - input/friend_bio.txt (friend’s name and disciplines)
   - input/art_pieces.csv (titles, tags, impact scores)
   - input/memories.jsonl (short memories to weave in)
   - input/brand_voice.md (tone and accessibility guidance)
   - input/platforms.yaml (character and hashtag limits)

2) Select the top 3 artworks by impact_score from input/art_pieces.csv. For each selected artwork, compose two posts: one for X and one for Instagram (6 posts total), following these constraints:
   - Include my friend’s name from input/friend_bio.txt in every post_text.
   - Include exactly one memory sentence taken verbatim from input/memories.jsonl for each artwork, chosen so at least one memory keyword overlaps that artwork’s tags. Do not reuse a memory across different artworks.
   - Hashtags must be derived from that artwork’s tags: lowercase, remove spaces/hyphens, prefixed with # (e.g., mixed-media -> #mixedmedia). No duplicate hashtags within a post.
   - Use platform limits from input/platforms.yaml for post_text length and maximum number of hashtags (X and Instagram limits differ).
   - Add one media item per post with file_name set to images/<artwork_slug>.jpg and alt_text that (a) mentions the artwork title and (b) includes at least one discipline from input/friend_bio.txt (e.g., “digital illustration”, “ink sketches”, or “mixed-media collages”).

3) Save the posts to output/posts/posts.json as a JSON array. Each item must have exactly these fields:
   - platform: "X" or "Instagram"
   - artwork_title: string (from CSV)
   - artwork_slug: string (from CSV)
   - post_text: string
   - hashtags: array of strings (e.g., ["#digital", "#portrait"]) within platform limits
   - media: array with one object: {"file_name": "images/<slug>.jpg", "alt_text": "..."}

4) Write a concise campaign summary to output/summary/campaign_summary.md with these sections and headings:
   - Objective: 1–2 sentences.
   - Selected Artworks: list the 3 titles with a brief reason referencing impact_score and tags.
   - Schedule: a simple 6-item listing (e.g., Day 1–Day 6) mapping each post (platform + artwork) to a day.
   - Status: include counts (6 posts total) and a line starting with "Voice:" that contains the words "appreciative" and "nostalgic" from input/brand_voice.md.

5) Add a simple validator script at scripts/validate.py and run it locally to produce output/validation/validation_report.txt. The validator must:
   - Load input/platforms.yaml and enforce per-platform max characters and max hashtags.
   - Confirm output/posts/posts.json exists, is valid JSON, and contains exactly 6 posts (2 per each of the top 3 artworks by impact_score from input/art_pieces.csv).
   - Check that each post_text includes the friend’s name from input/friend_bio.txt.
   - Verify that each post has non-empty media with file_name == images/<artwork_slug>.jpg and alt_text mentioning both the artwork title and at least one discipline from input/friend_bio.txt.
   - Ensure hashtags are lowercase tag-derivatives from the CSV (strip spaces/hyphens) and contain no duplicates within a post.
   - Confirm that each selected artwork’s posts include exactly one memory sentence, taken verbatim from input/memories.jsonl, and that no memory is reused for another artwork.
   - Verify output/summary/campaign_summary.md exists, includes all four required headings, and the Status section contains the words "Voice:", "appreciative", and "nostalgic".
   - Write a clear summary of pass/fail for each check to output/validation/validation_report.txt. If all checks pass, the first line of the report must be: ALL CHECKS PASSED.

Deliverables and paths:
- output/posts/posts.json (6 posts as specified)
- output/summary/campaign_summary.md (with the four sections)
- scripts/validate.py (the validator you wrote)
- output/validation/validation_report.txt (results after you run the validator locally)

Please do not use the Internet. Everything needed is in input/. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
